identity.short_name: Classify N/P/I for axis-placed points with two slope constraints

subdomain(s): coordinate geometry on axes and quadrants

problem_spec.summary: Points A,D lie on the x-axis and points B,C lie on the y-axis, with the origin between each pair. Two positive-slope constraints (AC and BD) force a small finite set of sign patterns. Five geometric propositions (slope sign, convexity, sign-contrast impossibility, concyclicity, and axis-tangent circle existence) are classified as necessarily true/possible/impossible. The output encodes the counts by 100 \cdot C(N)+10 \cdot C(P)+C(I).
givens: A=(a,0), D=(d,0) lie on the x-axis with a\neq0, d\neq0 and the origin lies strictly between them (ad<0).
B=(0,b), C=(0,c) lie on the y-axis with b\neq0, c\neq0 and the origin lies strictly between them (bc<0).
Five propositions (P1)-(P5) as stated in the Problem are to be classified as N/P/I.
Output code is out = 100 \cdot C(N)+10 \cdot C(P)+C(I).

parameter(s).name: axis_assignment
parameter(s).purpose: fixes which points lie on which axes (breaks the "either axis" symmetry).

parameter(s).name: between_origin_constraint
parameter(s).purpose: forces opposite rays for each paired axis.

parameter(s).name: slope_constraints
parameter(s).purpose: reduces admissible configurations to two sign-pattern families.

invariant(s).name: forced_sign_relations_from_slopes
invariant(s).statement: With A=(a,0), B=(0,b), C=(0,c), D=(d,0), the conditions slope(AC)>0 and slope(BD)>0 imply sign(b)=sign(a) and sign(c)=sign(d). Together with ad<0 and bc<0, this leaves exactly two global sign patterns related by (x,y)↦(-x,-y).

invariant(s).name: slope_AB_and_slope_CD_always_negative
invariant(s).statement: Under the forced sign relations, slope(AB)=-b/a<0 and slope(CD)=-c/d<0 in all admissible configurations.

invariant(s).name: concyclicity_condition_for_axis_points
invariant(s).statement: Points (a,0),(d,0),(0,b),(0,c) are concyclic iff ad = bc (equivalently, the constant term w in x^2+y^2+ux+vy+w=0 matches from x-axis and y-axis pairs).

invariant(s).name: axis_tangent_circle_axis_intersections
invariant(s).statement: A circle tangent to both coordinate axes meets each axis at exactly one point (the tangency point). Hence it passes through A=(a,0) and B=(0,b) iff |a|=|b| (and signs match the quadrant of the center).

invariant(s).name: convexity_by_axis_ray_order
invariant(s).statement: With A and B on rays of the same sign and C and D on rays of the opposite sign, the cyclic order A-B-D-C produces a simple quadrilateral; cross-product signs are consistent, so it is convex.
